title: Free Recall with Open-Loop Stimulation at Encoding
description: This dataset comprises intracranial electrophysiological recordings and behavioral data from a delayed free recall task with open-loop electrical brain stimulation during encoding. Participants studied word lists presented visually, performed distractor arithmetic tasks, and freely recalled words while stimulation was delivered to hippocampal and entorhinal cortex electrodes. The study involved multi-site clinical collaboration and systematically varied stimulation parameters across encoding blocks to investigate memory modulation.
methods_description: Intracranial electrophysiological recordings were collected during a delayed free recall task with open-loop stimulation at encoding. Participants studied 25-list sessions with 20 lists randomly assigned for stimulation. Electrical stimulation was delivered to single electrodes in hippocampus and entorhinal cortex during alternating two-word blocks (6 of 12 words stimulated per list). Stimulation parameters included amplitude, pulse frequency, pulse width, and pulse count, with onset 200 ms before word presentation and duration of 4.6 seconds. Recordings were acquired using Blackrock (250 nV units) and Medtronic (0.1 µV units) systems with monopolar (referenced) and bipolar configurations.
